message:
test(fcp-postgresql/transaction_integration): swap direct tokio for fcp_async_core (resolves br-zm14c)

Direct `tokio::*` usage in connectors/postgresql/tests/transaction_integration.rs
violated the runtime-hygiene policy that connectors should reach the
async runtime via `fcp_async_core` rather than tokio directly. Swap:

  - `use tokio::sync::Mutex;` → `use fcp_async_core::sync::Mutex;`
  - `tokio::spawn(driver_future)` → `fcp_async_core::task::spawn(driver_future)`
    (inherits the tokio compat handle so tokio_postgres' connection
    driver still has a tokio context to run on)
  - `tokio::spawn(serve_future)` → `fcp_async_core::task::spawn(serve_future)`
  - `tokio::time::sleep(...)` → `fcp_async_core::time::sleep(...)`
  - `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` →
    `#[fcp_async_core::runtime::test(flavor = "multi_thread")]`
    (the fcp_async_core macro accepts `flavor` but rejects
    `worker_threads`; flavor alone is the correct parity surface)

Kept as-is:
  - `tokio_postgres::{connect, NoTls, Client}` — the postgres driver
    crate that REQUIRES tokio internally; cannot be swapped without
    a different DB driver.
  - `tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(...)` — axum::serve takes a tokio
    TcpListener directly, so the bind stays on the tokio side; only
    the spawn that drives the serve task moves to fcp_async_core.

Test file is gated on `#[cfg(feature = "integration-testcontainer")]`
and requires Docker, so verification happens in CI rather than locally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
